CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Set For Trial On May-18-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp Motion)
Off Calendar Sep-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF JASPAL SINGH's Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp Motion) - The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted and denied in part. The commercial exception of CCP 425.17 does not apply to this action. CCP 425.16 does not apply to causes of action 1 and 2. Those causes of action are not based on petitioning activity. They allege that cross-defendant breached the parties' contractual agreement. Therefore, the motion is denied as to causes of action 1 and 2. The first prong of CCP 425.16 applies to causes of action 3 and 4. Cross-complainant demonstrates a probability of prevailing on cause of action 3. Cross-complainant presents evidence indicating that cross-defendant's complaint in this action has no merit and was filed as a means to scuttle the negotiations with Mr. Sheikh. Cross-defendant fails to show that the litigation privilege bars the cause of action. The court grants the motion as to cause of action 4. To prove defamation, cross-complainant must show that cross-defendant uttered the alleged defamatory remarks to a third party. Cross-complainant relies on paragraph 26 of the Rachi declaration. The court sustains cross-defendant's hearsay objection to that portion of the declaration. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/eg
